title: Publications | Ministry of Health NZ
content:
## Publications Ngā whakaputanga kōrero
- Consultation on the Code of Practice for Non-medical Irradiators: ORS C8 2020 Publication date: 16 June 2025 Last updated: 12 August 2025 The Director for Radiation Safety has reviewed the Code of Practice for Non-medical Irradiators: ORS C8 2020. The review was conducted in accordance with section 90 of the Radiation Safety Act 2016 (the Act). Consultation Radiation safety

## Consultation on the Code of Practice for Non-medical Irradiators: ORS C8 2020
The Director for Radiation Safety has reviewed the Code of Practice for Non-medical Irradiators: ORS C8 2020. The review was conducted in accordance with section 90 of the Radiation Safety Act 2016 (the Act).
- Strategy to Prevent and Minimise Gambling Harm 2025/26 to 2027/28 Publication date: 5 June 2025 The Strategy to Prevent and Minimise Gambling Harm 2025/26 to 2027/28 sets out the Government’s approach to, and funding for, services to prevent and minimise gambling harm during the three-year period 1 July 2025 to 30 June 2028. Publication Gambling harm

## Strategy to Prevent and Minimise Gambling Harm 2025/26 to 2027/28
The Strategy to Prevent and Minimise Gambling Harm 2025/26 to 2027/28 sets out the Government’s approach to, and funding for, services to prevent and minimise gambling harm during the three-year period 1 July 2025 to 30 June 2028.
- Models of Care for People Living with Long COVID Publication date: 29 May 2025 This paper reviews the current literature on models of care for people living with Long COVID. Publication COVID-19

## Models of Care for People Living with Long COVID
This paper reviews the current literature on models of care for people living with Long COVID.
- Gerontechnology: A Scoping Review of Telehealth and Ageing in Place Initiatives for Kaumātua with a Focus on Dementia and Loneliness Publication date: 19 May 2025 This brief was written by Rangimahora Reddy, the inaugural Ageing Well Fellow during her time working with the Office of the Chief Science Advisor as a part of the Ageing Well National Science Challenge. Publication Older people (65+) Health technology

## Gerontechnology: A Scoping Review of Telehealth and Ageing in Place Initiatives for Kaumātua with a Focus on Dementia and Loneliness
This brief was written by Rangimahora Reddy, the inaugural Ageing Well Fellow during her time working with the Office of the Chief Science Advisor as a part of the Ageing Well National Science Challenge.
- Consultation on Proposed Prescription Medicines List for Designated Podiatrist Prescribers: Analysis of Submissions Publication date: 15 May 2025 This document summarises feedback from the public consultation on the proposed prescription medicines list for designated podiatrist prescribers. Publication Medicines control Workforce

## Consultation on Proposed Prescription Medicines List for Designated Podiatrist Prescribers: Analysis of Submissions
This document summarises feedback from the public consultation on the proposed prescription medicines list for designated podiatrist prescribers.
- Greenhouse Gas Emissions for the 2023/24 Financial Year Report and Inventory Publication date: 1 May 2025 This document provides the report and inventory for the greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions of Ministry of Health for the financial year 2023/24 (1 July 2023 to 30 June 2024). Publication About the Ministry Environmental health

## Greenhouse Gas Emissions for the 2023/24 Financial Year Report and Inventory
This document provides the report and inventory for the greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions of Ministry of Health for the financial year 2023/24 (1 July 2023 to 30 June 2024).
- Environmental health
- Tupu Ola Moui : Pacific Health Chart Book 2025 series Publication date: 17 April 2025 Last updated: 17 July 2025 The Tupu Ola Moui series provides a snapshot on the state of Pacific health in Aotearoa New Zealand. Publication Pacific health Statistics Health system Workforce Public health

## Tupu Ola Moui : Pacific Health Chart Book 2025 series
The Tupu Ola Moui series provides a snapshot on the state of Pacific health in Aotearoa New Zealand.
- Review of Certain Matters under the Contraception, Sterilisation, and Abortion Act 1977 Publication date: 8 April 2025 In 2025 the Ministry of Health completed a review of certain matters under the Contraception, Sterilisation and Abortion Act 1977 as required by law. The review focused on timely and equitable access to services under the Act and was supported by data, feedback, research, and clinical guidance. Publication Abortion Sterilisation

## Review of Certain Matters under the Contraception, Sterilisation, and Abortion Act 1977
In 2025 the Ministry of Health completed a review of certain matters under the Contraception, Sterilisation and Abortion Act 1977 as required by law. The review focused on timely and equitable access to services under the Act and was supported by data, feedback, research, and clinical guidance.
- Abortion Services in Aotearoa New Zealand: The voices of wāhine on improving access Publication date: 8 April 2025 The research report focuses on the experiences of Māori and Pacific people and provides insights into how abortion health care services are perceived and accessed by this group since changes to the law in 2020. Publication Abortion Māori health Pacific health

## Abortion Services in Aotearoa New Zealand: The voices of wāhine on improving access
The research report focuses on the experiences of Māori and Pacific people and provides insights into how abortion health care services are perceived and accessed by this group since changes to the law in 2020.
- ‘It Just Adds Another Layer of Stress’: Rainbow people’s experiences of abortion services in Aotearoa New Zealand Publication date: 8 April 2025 The research report focuses on the experiences of rainbow people and provides insights into how abortion health care services are perceived and accessed by this group since changes to the law in 2020. Publication Abortion

## ‘It Just Adds Another Layer of Stress’: Rainbow people’s experiences of abortion services in Aotearoa New Zealand
The research report focuses on the experiences of rainbow people and provides insights into how abortion health care services are perceived and accessed by this group since changes to the law in 2020.
- Abortion Is a Human Right and Health Issue: Disabled people’s experiences of abortion services in Aotearoa New Zealand Publication date: 8 April 2025 The research report focuses on the experiences of disabled people and provides insights into how abortion health care services are perceived and accessed by this group since changes to the law in 2020. Publication Abortion Disability

## Abortion Is a Human Right and Health Issue: Disabled people’s experiences of abortion services in Aotearoa New Zealand
The research report focuses on the experiences of disabled people and provides insights into how abortion health care services are perceived and accessed by this group since changes to the law in 2020.
- Putting Patients First: Modernising health workforce regulation Publication date: 28 March 2025 This discussion document considers options for modernising New Zealand’s regulation of the health workforce. It supports public consultation on this topic. Consultation Health Practitioners Competence Assurance Act Workforce

## Putting Patients First: Modernising health workforce regulation
This discussion document considers options for modernising New Zealand’s regulation of the health workforce. It supports public consultation on this topic.
- Health Practitioners Competence Assurance Act
- Briefing to the Incoming Minister of Health January 2025 Publication date: 17 March 2025 This briefing provided the incoming Minister of Health, Hon Simeon Brown, with an overview of the context of recent Government reforms and priorities, and identified key issues and upcoming milestones and decisions for the Health Minister. Publication Health system

## Briefing to the Incoming Minister of Health January 2025
This briefing provided the incoming Minister of Health, Hon Simeon Brown, with an overview of the context of recent Government reforms and priorities, and identified key issues and upcoming milestones and decisions for the Health Minister.
- Strategic Approach to Immunisation in New Zealand 2025–2030 Publication date: 7 March 2025 This publication set the overarching vision for the immunisation system focusing on collective action and partnerships. It helps establish a shared understanding our immunisation objectives, align work across the system, and drive collective action towards a common goal. Publication Immunisation Public health

## Strategic Approach to Immunisation in New Zealand 2025–2030
This publication set the overarching vision for the immunisation system focusing on collective action and partnerships. It helps establish a shared understanding our immunisation objectives, align work across the system, and drive collective action towards a common goal.
- Te Awa Tārai – A career development guide for Allied Health - Hauora Haumi Publication date: 21 February 2025 Like a river, the flow of a career is influenced by the streams that feed it and the obstacles it meets. Te Awa Tārai - The River That Shapes was developed to support career development for allied health professionals in Aotearoa New Zealand. It is designed to acknowledge the varied and unconventional paths along which one’s career may flow. Publication Allied health

## Te Awa Tārai – A career development guide for Allied Health - Hauora Haumi
Like a river, the flow of a career is influenced by the streams that feed it and the obstacles it meets. Te Awa Tārai - The River That Shapes was developed to support career development for allied health professionals in Aotearoa New Zealand. It is designed to acknowledge the varied and unconventional paths along which one’s career may flow.
